options.title: Physics
options.description: Students may substitute courses from the Physics 1 honors series for the Physics 1 series.
courses: PHYSICS 1A - Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Mechanics
PHYSICS 1B - Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Oscillations, Waves, Electric and Magnetic Fields
PHYSICS 1C - Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Electrodynamics, Optics, and Special Relativity
PHYSICS 4AL - Physics Laboratory for Scientists and Engineers: Mechanics
PHYSICS 4BL - Physics Laboratory for Scientists and Engineers: Electricity and Magnetism

options.title: Physics 1 Honors series
courses: PHYSICS 1AH - Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Mechanics (Honors)
PHYSICS 1BH - Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Oscillations, Waves, Electric and Magnetic Fields (Honors)
PHYSICS 1CH - Physics for Scientists and Engineers: Electrodynamics, Optics, and Special Relativity (Honors)

options.title: Mathematics
courses: MATH 31AL - Differential and Integral Calculus Laboratory

options.title: Program in Computing or Computer Science
courses: COM SCI 30 - Principles and Practices of Computing
COM SCI 31 - Introduction to Computer Science I

options.title: Plan
options.description: It is preferred that the three additional upper-division courses for the plan are selected from:

options.title: Upper-Division Electives
courses: PHYSICS 114 - Mechanics of Wave Motion and Sound
PHYSICS M122 - Introduction to Plasma Science and Engineering
PHYSICS 150 - Physics of Charged-Particle and Laser Beams
PHYSICS C187A - Biological Physics I: Life at Rest

options.title: Preparation for Graduate School
courses: PHYSICS 114 - Mechanics of Wave Motion and Sound
PHYSICS M122 - Introduction to Plasma Science and Engineering

json_data.title: Honors Program
json_data.description: Students must complete two 4-unit terms of Physics 199.

options.title: Research-Based
courses: PHYSICS 199 - Directed Research or Senior Project in Physics
